page: Google Cloud Platform ct5lp-hightpu-4t is an accelerator-optimized server instance designed for parallel computing and machine learning workloads. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides approximately 1.71 GB of RAM per core, indicating a lean memory-to-core ratio optimized for high-throughput computation rather than memory-intensive database caching.

The primary hardware feature of this instance is the inclusion of four integrated ct5lp Google Tensor Processing Units. These specialized hardware accelerators are designed to handle complex matrix operations. The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ct5lp-hightpu-4t consolidates significant accelerator capacity and a high vCPU count into a single node, offering a dense resource profile for specialized workloads. This design is highly suited for machine learning training, deep learning inference, and large-scale mathematical simulations that leverage TPU-optimized frameworks.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct5lp-hightpu-4t is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 112 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory. The primary differentiator of this instance is the integration of four ct5lp Google Tensor Processing Units, which provide specialized hardware acceleration for matrix operations. The system features a lean memory-to-core ratio of 1.71 GB per vCPU and does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. It also provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is qualitatively structured for resource density, offering a high ratio of accelerator units to host resources. The ct5lp-hightpu-4t is designed for workloads such as machine learning model training, deep learning inference, and parallel mathematical simulations optimized for TPU architectures.
